arts.ttl: Barcelona enter free fall, whereas Atlético Madrid flex title credentials
arts.sum: Barcelona's inconsistency continues as Atlético Madrid's last-second 2-1 win over the Blaugrana see them move atop of LaLiga.

arts.ttl: What can Barcelona learn from painful Atletico defeat?
arts.sum: When the momentum shifts, or if the opponent scores a goal against the run of play, like Atletico Madrid did late, or as Leganes did early, Barcelona doesn’t have a response. The year has mercifully come to an end for Hansi Flick and Barcelona.

arts.ttl: Flick on late Barça defeat: We need the break
arts.sum: Hansi Flick said Barcelona need a break after Atlético Madrid inflicted a third straight home league defeat on them to replace them at the top of LaLiga on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Barcelona set 'expiry date' for attacker sale as 22-year-old hits transfer crossroads
arts.sum: Barcelona want to sever ties with attacker Ansu Fati at the earliest opportunity in the January transfer window, according to a report.

arts.ttl: ‘Very tough defeat’ - Pedri rues Barcelona’s missed chances
arts.sum: Barcelona midfielder Pedri admitted Saturday’s defeat to Atletico was tough to take after seeing the Catalans beaten 2-1 at home in La Liga . We had chances to close the game and when you don’t close it out, what happens happens.

arts.ttl: Hansi Flick has no issues with performance despite Atletico Madrid loss
arts.sum: Barcelona boss Hansi Flick offered a passionate response to their 2-1 La Liga loss at home to Atletico Madrid. La Blaugrana end 2024 knocked off top spot in the Spanish top-flight, with just one win in their last seven league games, as Flick assesses his options. arts.ttl: Stat: Barcelona’s alarming dependence on Lamine Yamal highlighted again in Atlético defeat
arts.sum: A head-turning statistic, highlighting the ongoing struggles of Barcelona in the absence of Lamine Yamal, has tonight come to the fore online. This comes owing to developments in Barca’s La…

arts.ttl: Barcelona 1-2 Atletico Madrid: Player Ratings From Atlético's Shocking Stoppage Time Victory
arts.sum: Atletico Madrid defeated Barcelona in a thrilling match and will end the year atop the standings in La Liga.

arts.ttl: Alexander Sørloth stuns Barcelona with last-gasp winner for Atlético Madrid
arts.athr: Sid Lowe at the Estadio Olímpic Lluís Companys
arts.sum: They were 95 minutes into the battle that been built as the clash that would decide the title, the final seconds slipping away, when a man who looks for all the world like a norse warrior delivered the decisive blow. Atlético Madrid had suffered, they had resisted and, in truth, they had probably longed for that final whistle to go, and then suddenly Alexander Sørloth thumped in another late winner. In doing so he sent Atlético Madrid top the table at Christmas and their coach, staff and subs sprinting on to the pitch. Barcelona’s fans, meanwhile, turned and headed straight for the exit, unable to believe what had just happened. What had happened was this: on a night when they had hit the bar and had more than enough chances to win it, including deep into added time, in which Jan Oblak, the goalkeeper had been the best player on the pitch, with Pedri, they had somehow been beaten. It is the third time in a row that they have lost here in La Liga and the first time they have ever fallen at the hands of a Diego Simeone-led Atlético team and it hurt. Continue reading...
